title: pilosella procerigena
intro: Pilosella procerigena, also known as a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family, is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Central and Eastern Europe. Traditionally, Pilosella procerigena has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating joint pain due to its high tannin content. Pilosella procerigena can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can be dried and used in herbal remedies.
